content:
# 2 Corinthians Chapter 4 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2CO_004_page_001.jpg

1. Therefore, having this ministry, as we received mercy, we do not lose heart,

2. but we have renounced the hidden [things] of shame, not walking in craftiness nor adulterating the word of God, but by the manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every conscience of people before God.

3. And if indeed the good news of us is veiled, it is veiled in the [ones] perishing,

4. in whom the god of this age has blinded the minds of the unbelieving, so that the light of the good news of the glory of the Christos, who is [the] image of God, might not dawn [upon them].

5. For we do not proclaim ourselves, but Christos Yeshua [as] Lord, and ourselves [as] your slaves because of Yeshua.

6. Because [it is] the God who said, "Out of darkness light shall shine," who shone in the hearts of us for [the] illumination of the knowledge of the glory of God in [the] face of Yeshua Christos.

7. And we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the surpassing [greatness] of the power may be of God and not from us.

8. In every [way] being afflicted but not crushed, being perplexed but not despairing,

9. being persecuted but not forsaken, being struck down but not destroyed,

10. always carrying around in the body the dying of the Yeshua, that also the life of the Yeshua may be manifested in the body of us.

11. For always we the living are being handed over to death because of Yeshua, that also the life of the Yeshua may be manifested in the mortal flesh of us.

12. So then death works in us, but life in you.

13. And having the same spirit of faith according to that which has been written, "I believed, therefore I spoke," we also believe, therefore also we speak,

14. knowing that the [one] having raised the Lord Yeshua will also raise us with Yeshua and will present [us] with you.

15. For all [things] [are] for your sake, that the grace having increased through the many may cause the thanksgiving to abound to the glory of God.

16. Therefore we do not lose heart. But even if the outer person of us is being destroyed, yet the inner [person] of us is being renewed day by day.

17. For the momentary lightness of the affliction of us is producing for us an eternal weight of glory beyond all measure,

18. [we] not looking at the [things] being seen but at the [things] not being seen. For the [things] being seen [are] temporary, but the [things] not being seen [are] eternal.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.4 — "the god of this age":** ὁ θεὸς τοῦ αἰῶνος τούτου — Satan as the ruler of the present age. This is the only place in the NT where Satan is called "god" (θεός).

2. **v.4 — "image of God":** εἰκὼν τοῦ θεοῦ — Christ as the visible representation of the invisible God (cf. Col 1:15). The term εἰκών implies both representation and participation.

3. **v.6 — "Out of darkness light shall shine":** Echoes Genesis 1:3 (creation of light). Paul connects the original creation to the new creation in Christ — the same God who created light now shines in human hearts.

4. **v.7 — "earthen vessels":** ὀστρακίνοις σκεύεσιν — cheap clay pots, the most disposable containers in the ancient world. The contrast between treasure (gospel) and container (apostle) is the key to Paul's theology of weakness.

5. **v.10 — "the dying of Yeshua":** τὴν νέκρωσιν τοῦ Ἰησοῦ — νέκρωσις means "dying" or "putting to death" (a process), not θάνατος ("death" as event). Paul carries Christ's dying in his body — his suffering is a participation in Christ's death.

6. **v.17 — "momentary lightness...eternal weight of glory":** A wordplay: βάρος (weight/heaviness) of glory contrasts with ἐλαφρόν (lightness) of affliction. In Hebrew, כָּבוֹד (kavod, "glory") derives from כָּבֵד (kaved, "heavy"). Paul may be playing on the Hebrew etymology.

— EMET, AI Translator
